payload.message: The board exposed one semantic gap from the migration: with `pending_process_verification: true`, the pre-refresh done tickets should not still read as `trusted`. I’m correcting that now so the human-facing queue matches the workflow contract instead of implying the reverification window is already closed.

content.text: The board exposed one semantic gap from the migration: with `pending_process_verification: true`, the pre-refresh done tickets should not still read as `trusted`. I’m correcting that now so the human-facing queue matches the workflow contract instead of implying the reverification window is already closed.

payload.name: exec_command
payload.arguments:
{"cmd":"python3 - <<'PY'
import json, re
from pathlib import Path

root = Path('/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker')
manifest_path = root / 'tickets/manifest.json'
manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text())

for ticket in manifest['tickets']:
    if ticket.get('status') == 'done':
        ticket['verification_state'] = 'suspect'

manifest_path.write_text(json.dumps(manifest, indent=2) + '\n', encoding='utf-8')


def extract_notes(path: Path) -> str:
    if not path.exists():
        return ''
    text = path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    match = re.search(r'\n## Notes\n\n?([\s\S]*)$', text)
    return match.group(1).rstrip() if match else ''


def render_artifact_line(artifact: dict) -> str:
    summary = f" - {artifact['summary']}" if artifact.get('summary') else ''
    trust = f" [{artifact['trust_state']}]" if artifact.get('trust_state') and artifact['trust_state'] != 'current' else ''
    return f"- {artifact['kind']}: {artifact['path']} ({artifact['stage']}){trust}{summary}"


def render_ticket(ticket: dict, notes: str) -> str:
    depends_on = ', '.join(ticket.get('depends_on', [])) if ticket.get('depends_on') else 'None'
    blockers = '\n'.join(f"- {item}" for item in ticket.get('decision_blockers', [])) if ticket.get('decision_blockers') else 'None'
    acceptance = '\n'.join(f"- [ ] {item}" for item in ticket.get('acceptance', [])) if ticket.get('acceptance') else 'None'
    follow_ups = '\n'.join(f"- {item}" for item in ticket.get('follow_up_ticket_ids', [])) if ticket.get('follow_up_ticket_ids') else 'None'
    artifacts = '\n'.join(render_artifact_line(item) for item in ticket.get('artifacts', [])) if ticket.get('artifacts') else '- None yet'
    note_block = f"{notes.rstrip()}\n" if notes.rstrip() else ''
    return (
        f"# {ticket['id']}: {ticket['title']}\n\n"
        f"## Summary\n\n{ticket['summary']}\n\n"
        f"## Wave\n\n{ticket['wave']}\n\n"
        f"## Lane\n\n{ticket['lane']}\n\n"
        f"## Parallel Safety\n\n"
        f"- parallel_safe: {'true' if ticket.get('parallel_safe') else 'false'}\n"
        f"- overlap_risk: {ticket['overlap_risk']}\n\n"
        f"## Stage\n\n{ticket['stage']}\n\n"
        f"## Status\n\n{ticket['status']}\n\n"
        f"## Trust\n\n"
        f"- resolution_state: {ticket['resolution_state']}\n"
        f"- verification_state: {ticket['verification_state']}\n"
        f"- source_ticket_id: {ticket.get('source_ticket_id') or 'None'}\n"
        f"- source_mode: {ticket.get('source_mode') or 'None'}\n\n"
        f"## Depends On\n\n{depends_on}\n\n"
        f"## Follow-up Tickets\n\n{follow_ups}\n\n"
        f"## Decision Blockers\n\n{blockers}\n\n"
        f"## Acceptance Criteria\n\n{acceptance}\n\n"
        f"## Artifacts\n\n{artifacts}\n\n"
        f"## Notes\n\n{note_block}"
    )

rows = []
for ticket in manifest['tickets']:
    depends_on = ', '.join(ticket.get('depends_on', [])) if ticket.get('depends_on') else '-'
    follow_ups = ', '.join(ticket.get('follow_up_ticket_ids', [])) if ticket.get('follow_up_ticket_ids') else '-'
    rows.append(
        f"| {ticket['wave']} | {ticket['id']} | {ticket['title']} | {ticket['lane']} | {ticket['stage']} | {ticket['status']} | {ticket['resolution_state']} | {ticket['verification_state']} | {'yes' if ticket.get('parallel_safe') else 'no'} | {ticket['overlap_risk']} | {depends_on} | {follow_ups} |"
    )
board = '# Ticket Board\n\n| Wave | ID | Title | Lane | Stage | Status | Resolution | Verification | Parallel Safe | Overlap Risk | Depends On | Follow-ups |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n' + '\n'.join(rows) + '\n'
(root / 'tickets/BOARD.md').write_text(board, encoding='utf-8')
for ticket in manifest['tickets']:
    path = root / 'tickets' / f"{ticket['id']}.md"
    path.write_text(render_ticket(ticket, extract_notes(path)), encoding='utf-8')
PY","workdir":"/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker","max_output_tokens":12000,"yield_time_ms":1000}

# FIX-017: Clean up duplicate endpoints, response models, and artifact registry

## Summary

Three cleanup items: (1) Duplicate /health endpoint in main.py and routes.py. (2) health_check returns raw dict instead of HealthResponse Pydantic model. (3) Duplicate SETUP-002 artifact in registry.json.

## Wave

8

## Lane

hardening

## Parallel Safety

- parallel_safe: true
- overlap_risk: low

## Stage

closeout

## Status

done

## Trust

- resolution_state: done
- verification_state: suspect
- source_ticket_id: None
- source_mode: None

## Depends On

FIX-003

## Follow-up Tickets

None

## Decision Blockers

None

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Single /health endpoint in routes.py
- [ ] Health endpoint returns HealthResponse Pydantic model
- [ ] No duplicate artifact entries in registry.json
- [ ] Consistent kind field values across all artifact entries

## Artifacts

- plan: .opencode/state/plans/fix-017-planning-plan.md (planning) - Implementation plan for FIX-017: Clean up duplicate endpoints, response models, and artifact registry. Defines steps to remove duplicate /health from main.py, add proper /health to routes.py with HealthResponse, remove duplicate SETUP-002 artifact from registry, and standardize kind field values.
- review: .opencode/state/plans/fix-017-planning-review.md (planning) - Plan review for FIX-017: APPROVED - all findings verified against source files
- implementation: .opencode/state/implementations/fix-017-implementation-implementation.md (implementation) - Implementation of FIX-017: Removed duplicate /health endpoint from main.py, added proper /health endpoint to routes.py with HealthResponse Pydantic model, removed duplicate SETUP-002 artifact from registry.json, and standardized all kind field values from "plan" to "planning".
- qa: .opencode/state/qa/fix-017-qa-qa.md (qa) - QA verification for FIX-017: All 4 acceptance criteria PASS - single /health endpoint in routes.py, HealthResponse Pydantic model used, no duplicate SETUP-002 entries, consistent kind field values
- smoke-test: .opencode/state/smoke-tests/fix-017-smoke-test-smoke-test.md (smoke-test) - PASS (compileall passed, pytest failed due to pre-existing aiosqlite environment issue)

## Notes

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/START-HERE.md
@@
 ## Current State
 
-All implementation complete. Every ticket across waves 0–8 (55 tickets) is at stage `closeout`, status `done`. The codebase covers:
+Core product implementation across waves 0–8 remains in place, but the March 24, 2026 Scafforge audit opened a v5 reverification window and added 2 wave 9 remediation tickets. The codebase still covers:
 - Hub core: MCP tool routing, policy engine, node registry
 - Node agent service
 - Repo inspection tools (`list_nodes`, `list_repos`, `inspect_repo_tree`, `read_repo_file`, `search_repo`, `git_status`)
@@
 ## Process Contract
 
-- process_version: 4
+- process_version: 5
 - parallel_mode: parallel-lanes
-- pending_process_verification: false
-- process_changed_at: 2026-03-20T15:39:45Z
-- process_state: No pending process-change verification.
+- pending_process_verification: true
+- process_changed_at: 2026-03-24T23:43:12Z
+- process_state: Pre-refresh done tickets are not fully trusted until backlog verification closes the Scafforge v5 verification window.
@@
 ## Current Or Next Ticket
 
-No active ticket. All wave 0–8 tickets are done.
+Active ticket: `EXEC-001` — stage `planning`, status `ready`.
 
-Recommended next steps (create new tickets if needed):
-- Deploy hub + node agents in a real Tailscale environment and verify end-to-end ChatGPT → MCP → hub → node flow
-- Resolve open questions from `docs/spec/CANONICAL-BRIEF.md` section 9 (embedding model, Qdrant deployment mode, Cloudflare domain config)
-- Add wave 9 tickets for any gaps found during deployment
+Immediate follow-up:
+- `EXEC-001`: fix the node-agent FastAPI dependency injection import failure in [`src/node_agent/dependencies.py`](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/src/node_agent/dependencies.py)
+- `EXEC-002`: rerun collection and the full test suite after `EXEC-001` lands
+- Reverify historical done tickets before treating waves 0–8 as fully trusted under process v5
@@
 ## Validation Status
 
-`repo-process-doctor` audit run 2026-03-23: **0 findings**. Workflow contract is clean and aligned with Scafforge process v4.
+`scafforge-audit` run on March 24, 2026 validated 2 execution failures:
+- `.venv/bin/python -c "from src.node_agent.main import app"` fails with `fastapi.exceptions.FastAPIError` from [`src/node_agent/dependencies.py`](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/src/node_agent/dependencies.py)
+- `.venv/bin/pytest tests/ --collect-only -q --tb=no` stops at [`tests/node_agent/test_executor.py`](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/tests/node_agent/test_executor.py) because the same node-agent import path is broken
@@
 ## Known Risks
 
+- Pre-refresh done tickets remain `suspect` until backlog verification closes the current process-change window
+- The node agent does not import cleanly in the repo virtualenv
+- The pytest suite cannot complete while the node-agent import path is broken
 - Embedding model for Qdrant not yet chosen — context indexing requires a concrete model before first `index_repo` call
 - Qdrant deployment mode (embedded vs. standalone) unresolved — affects hub resource footprint
 - Cloudflare Tunnel domain and access policy details not configured — required for ChatGPT MCP connectivity
-- Git hook integration for indexing triggers not wired up — scheduled fallback is the only auto-trigger until resolved
 - No live Tailscale environment test yet — all validation is unit/contract-level
 
 ## Next Action
 
-Deploy and smoke-test the hub. Run `make` or `pytest` to confirm the test suite is green, then stand up a hub + one node agent against a real Tailscale tailnet. Resolve section 9 open questions from `docs/spec/CANONICAL-BRIEF.md` before first production use.
+Plan and implement `EXEC-001`, then run `.venv/bin/pytest tests/ --collect-only -q --tb=no` and `.venv/bin/pytest tests/ -q --tb=no` to clear `EXEC-002`. After the source-layer fix is proven, route the backlog verifier across the historical done tickets that predate the March 24, 2026 process refresh.
 <!-- SCAFFORGE:START_HERE_BLOCK END -->
*** End Patch

# GPTTalker — START HERE

<!-- SCAFFORGE:START_HERE_BLOCK START -->
## What This Repo Is

GPTTalker is a lightweight MCP hub that lets ChatGPT safely interact with a multi-machine development environment. ChatGPT sends requests via MCP, the hub enforces policy, routes work to the right machine over Tailscale, and returns structured results. Features include repo inspection, LLM bridging, markdown delivery, persistent project context with semantic search (Qdrant), cross-repo intelligence, distributed model routing, and full observability.

Stack: Python 3.11+ / FastAPI / SQLite / Qdrant / Tailscale / Cloudflare Tunnel.
Agent model: `minimax-coding-plan/MiniMax-M2.5` for all agents.

## Current State

Core product implementation across waves 0–8 remains in place, but the March 24, 2026 Scafforge audit opened a v5 reverification window and added 2 wave 9 remediation tickets. The codebase still covers:
- Hub core: MCP tool routing, policy engine, node registry
- Node agent service
- Repo inspection tools (`list_nodes`, `list_repos`, `inspect_repo_tree`, `read_repo_file`, `search_repo`, `git_status`)
- Markdown delivery (`write_markdown` with atomic writes)
- LLM bridge (`chat_llm`, OpenCode adapter, helper/embedding adapters)
- Context system (`index_repo`, `get_project_context`, Qdrant indexing, issue tracking)
- Cross-repo intelligence (`search_across_repos`, `get_architecture_map`)
- Distributed scheduler (`route_task`, node selection, fallback)
- Observability (`list_task_history`, `get_task_details`, `list_generated_docs`, `get_issue_timeline`)
- Cloudflare Tunnel public edge
- Full contract and security test suites (waves 6–8 hardening)

## Process Contract

- process_version: 5
- parallel_mode: parallel-lanes
- pending_process_verification: true
- process_changed_at: 2026-03-24T23:43:12Z

## Findings

### [error] EXEC001

Problem: One or more Python packages fail to import — the service cannot start.
Root cause: Runtime errors (NameError, FastAPIError, missing dependency, broken DI pattern, etc.) that are invisible to static analysis prevent module load. Common causes: TYPE_CHECKING-guarded names used in runtime annotations, FastAPI dependency functions with non-Pydantic parameter types, circular imports.
Files:
- /home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/src
Target safer pattern: Verify every import succeeds: `python -c 'from src.<pkg>.main import app'`. Use string annotations (`-> "TypeName"`) for TYPE_CHECKING-only imports. Use `request: Request` (not `app: FastAPI`) in FastAPI dependency functions.

payload.message:
Applied the audit/repair flow and removed the temporary diagnosis pack afterward.

The repo is now on Scafforge process v5 with the workflow state and provenance updated in [workflow-state.json](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/.opencode/state/workflow-state.json) and [bootstrap-provenance.json](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/.opencode/meta/bootstrap-provenance.json). I refreshed the scaffold-managed commands/tools/plugins/docs, aligned [AGENTS.md](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/AGENTS.md) and [START-HERE.md](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/START-HERE.md), upgraded the ticket/artifact contract to the newer shape, and marked historical done tickets as `suspect` while `pending_process_verification` is open. I also completed the missing execution-enforcement prompt update in [gpttalker-implementer-context.md](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/.opencode/agents/gpttalker-implementer-context.md).

The audit still validates 2 source-layer failures, so I routed them into new repo tickets instead of changing product code inside the repair pass: [EXEC-001.md](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/tickets/EXEC-001.md) for the node-agent FastAPI dependency injection import failure and [EXEC-002.md](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/tickets/EXEC-002.md) for restoring pytest collection/full execution after that fix. [BOARD.md](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/tickets/BOARD.md) and [manifest.json](/home/pc/projects/GPTTalker/tickets/manifest.json) were regenerated to reflect that state.

Verification: `python3 /home/pc/.codex/skills/scafforge-audit/scripts/audit_repo_process.py /home/pc/projects/GPTTalker --format both --fail-on warning` still exits with the expected remaining findings `EXEC001` and `EXEC002`; no extra workflow-contract findings were introduced by the repair.
